description_long: This is a mid-sized passenger jet painted primarily white with red and gray accents, featuring the Polish national insignia and the text 'RZECZPOSPOLITA POLSKA' along the fuselage. The airplane has a low-wing configuration with straight wings and red-tipped winglets, and two jet engines mounted at the rear of the fuselage. The tail is a T-tail design, with the horizontal stabilizer mounted atop the vertical fin. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple passenger windows running along the sides. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it may be retractable.
description: A white and red Polish passenger jet with straight low wings, rear-mounted engines, and a T-tail design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the straight wings with red-tipped winglets and the nose of the airplane.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the full length of the fuselage, passenger windows, and Polish markings.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the T-tail, rear-mounted engines, and symmetrical wing structure.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the tail section, engine placement, and national insignia.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and small red-tipped winglets; no visible dihedral or anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
